hypothesis: If ML-based evaluation metrics for generative materials models lack grounding in physical simulation, then they will fail to accurately capture true structural stability and chemical validity, because metrics derived from DFT-based relaxation and thermodynamic convex hull analysis reflect the actual energy landscape governing material existence, whereas purely statistical or fingerprint-based metrics can be systematically deceived by structures that appear reasonable in latent space but are physically unstable or chemically implausible.
